set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: di cư, sự di chuyển của dân số từ nơi này sang nơi khác
definition_en: the movement of people from one place to another, often to seek better opportunities
example_sentence: Immigration can help offset the effects of an aging population by bringing younger workers.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: chảy máu chất xám, sự di cư của những người có trình độ cao sang nước khác
definition_en: the emigration of highly educated or skilled people from their home country
example_sentence: Brain drain exacerbates workforce shortages in aging societies losing young professionals.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: hệ thống trả theo từng kỳ, hệ thống lương hưu mà người lao động hiện tại trả cho người hưu trí
definition_en: a pension system where current workers' contributions directly fund current retirees' benefits
example_sentence: Pay-as-you-go systems become unsustainable when the ratio of workers to retirees declines sharply.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: chăm sóc dài hạn, dịch vụ hỗ trợ sinh hoạt và y tế cho người mất khả năng tự chủ
definition_en: ongoing support services for individuals who cannot manage daily activities independently
example_sentence: Long-term care facilities are becoming increasingly necessary as life expectancy extends.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: cơ hội nhân khẩu, giai đoạn khi dân số trẻ tạo lợi thế kinh tế
definition_en: economic growth potential when a large proportion of the population is of working age
example_sentence: India's demographic dividend is expected to drive economic growth for the next two decades.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: gánh nặng chăm sóc, áp lực tâm lý và vật lý của người chăm sóc người già
definition_en: the physical, emotional, and financial stress experienced by those caring for elderly or ill relatives
example_sentence: The caregiver burden increases significantly in societies where family-based elderly care is the norm.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: lão hóa tích cực, khuyến khích người cao tuổi tiếp tục hoạt động và đóng góp xã hội
definition_en: policies and practices promoting continued engagement and contribution of older adults in society
example_sentence: Active aging programs encourage older workers to remain in the workforce longer.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: phân biệt đối xử theo tuổi, sự bất công trong tuyển dụng hoặc thăng chức dựa trên tuổi
definition_en: unfair treatment of individuals based on their age, particularly in employment
example_sentence: Age discrimination in hiring practices prevents older workers from remaining in the labor force.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: kim tự tháp dân số, biểu đồ thể hiện cơ cấu tuổi và giới tính của dân số
definition_en: a graphical representation showing the age and gender distribution of a population
example_sentence: Japan's population pyramid has inverted, showing more elderly than young people.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: mức thay thế, tỷ suất sinh cần thiết để dân số ổn định (khoảng 2.1 con/phụ nữ)
definition_en: the fertility rate required to maintain a stable population without migration
example_sentence: Most developed nations have fallen below replacement level, leading to natural population decline.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: y học lão khoa, chuyên ngành y tế chuyên điều trị bệnh ở người cao tuổi
definition_en: the medical specialty focused on diagnosis and treatment of diseases in elderly patients
example_sentence: The demand for geriatric medicine specialists is growing rapidly in aging societies.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: kết dính xã hội, mức độ liên kết và hợp tác giữa các thành viên xã hội
definition_en: the degree of unity and cooperation among members of a society
example_sentence: Intergenerational tensions can threaten social cohesion when young workers resent supporting retirees.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: suy giảm năng suất, giảm hiệu quả sản xuất do lao động già hoặc thiếu hụt lao động
definition_en: a decrease in economic output per worker or per unit of input
example_sentence: Productivity decline threatens economic competitiveness in countries with rapidly aging workforces.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: hộ gia đình đa thế hệ, gia đình sống chung gồm nhiều thế hệ khác nhau
definition_en: a household containing members from three or more generations living together
example_sentence: Multigenerational households are becoming more common as families seek to share elderly care costs.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: gánh nặng kinh tế, chi phí lớn gây áp lực lên ngân sách hoặc tài chính
definition_en: a significant financial cost or obligation that strains economic resources
example_sentence: The economic burden of supporting an aging population is reshaping government budgets worldwide.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: góc nhìn vòng đời, cách tiếp cận xem xét sự phát triển con người qua các giai đoạn
definition_en: an analytical approach examining how experiences across different life stages shape outcomes
example_sentence: A life-course perspective reveals how early employment decisions affect retirement security.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: cải cách chính sách, thay đổi các quy định để thích ứng với tình hình mới
definition_en: changes to government policies designed to address emerging challenges or improve outcomes
example_sentence: Comprehensive policy reform is necessary to ensure pension systems remain viable for future generations.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: quá trình chuyển đổi từ tỷ lệ sinh và tử vong cao sang thấp, đặc trưng của sự phát triển kinh tế-xã hội
definition_en: the shift from high birth and death rates to low rates as a society develops economically
example_sentence: East Asian countries experienced rapid demographic transition over just three decades, creating unprecedented policy challenges.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: tình trạng số dân giảm do tỷ lệ sinh thấp hơn tỷ lệ tử vong, hoặc di cư ròng âm
definition_en: a decline in total population due to low birth rates exceeding death rates or net emigration
example_sentence: Japan's shrinking population has forced policymakers to reconsider immigration policies and automate manufacturing sectors.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: tình trạng người cao tuổi phụ thuộc vào những người đang làm việc để hỗ trợ kinh tế
definition_en: the economic reliance of elderly people on the working-age population for financial and social support
example_sentence: Rising old-age dependency in Europe threatens the sustainability of public pension schemes without significant tax increases.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: tỷ lệ sinh thấp hơn mức cần thiết để duy trì dân số ổn định (dưới 2,1 con/phụ nữ)
definition_en: a birth rate below the level needed to maintain a stable population without immigration
example_sentence: Most developed nations now experience sub-replacement fertility, necessitating immigration to sustain economic growth.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: các ngành kinh tế và dịch vụ phục vụ người cao tuổi, bao gồm y tế, du lịch, công nghệ hỗ trợ
definition_en: economic sectors and services targeting the elderly population, including healthcare, leisure, and assistive technology
example_sentence: The silver economy is projected to grow faster than traditional sectors, attracting significant venture capital investment.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: sự chuyển giao tài sản, kiến thức, hoặc trách nhiệm từ thế hệ này sang thế hệ khác
definition_en: the transmission of wealth, knowledge, or responsibilities from one generation to the next
example_sentence: Intergenerational transfer of property and savings remains a critical mechanism for wealth inequality in aging societies.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: tình trạng có tỷ lệ cao người trẻ tuổi trong dân số, thường gặp ở các nước đang phát triển
definition_en: a demographic pattern where a large proportion of the population is young, common in developing countries
example_sentence: Sub-Saharan Africa's youth bulge presents both opportunities for economic growth and challenges for job creation.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: rủi ro tài chính khi con người sống lâu hơn dự kiến, làm cạn kiệt tiền hưu trí hoặc quỹ bảo hiểm
definition_en: the financial risk that individuals or institutions face when people live longer than expected, depleting retirement savings
example_sentence: Insurance companies and pension funds must carefully manage longevity risk through actuarial modeling and product innovation.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: nhóm dân số trong độ tuổi có khả năng lao động, thường từ 15-64 tuổi
definition_en: the segment of population capable of working, typically aged 15-64 years
synonyms: economically active population, labor force age group
example_sentence: The working-age population in Europe is projected to decline by 10% over the next two decades.

set_name: Dân số già & Biến đổi nhân khẩu
definition_vi: áp lực tài chính chính phủ do chi tiêu tăng (y tế, hưu trí) vượt quá doanh thu thuế
definition_en: government financial strain caused by rising expenditures (healthcare, pensions) exceeding tax revenues
example_sentence: Aging populations create mounting fiscal pressure on governments, forcing difficult choices between taxation and benefit reductions.

definition_vi: bệnh mãn tính, bệnh kéo dài lâu năm
definition_en: a long-lasting condition that usually progresses slowly, such as diabetes or heart disease
example_sentence: Regular exercise and balanced nutrition are crucial for managing chronic diseases and improving quality of life.

definition_vi: chẩn đoán, xác định bệnh
definition_en: the identification of the nature of an illness or other problem by examination of the symptoms
example_sentence: An early diagnosis of cancer significantly increases the patient's chances of successful treatment and recovery.

definition_vi: tác dụng phụ không tốt, tác dụng tiêu cực
definition_en: an undesirable or harmful reaction caused by a drug or medical treatment
example_sentence: Patients must be informed about potential adverse effects before commencing any new medication or treatment.

definition_vi: đơn thuốc, chỉ định của bác sĩ
definition_en: a written instruction from a doctor to take a particular medicine or drug
example_sentence: The pharmacist carefully reviewed the prescription to ensure the correct dosage and medication were dispensed to the patient.

definition_vi: tiêm chủng, tạo miễn dịch
definition_en: to treat a person with a vaccine to protect them against a particular disease
example_sentence: Children should be immunized against measles and whooping cough before entering primary school to protect public health.

definition_vi: suy giảm, trở nên tệ hơn
definition_en: to become progressively worse, especially regarding health or a medical condition
example_sentence: Without proper treatment, the patient's respiratory condition continued to deteriorate rapidly throughout the hospital admission.

definition_vi: dự đoán tiên lượng bệnh
definition_en: a forecast of the likely course and outcome of a disease, especially regarding the chances of recovery
example_sentence: The cardiologist provided a detailed prognosis, explaining that early intervention significantly improved the patient's long-term survival rate.

definition_vi: có biểu hiện bệnh, có triệu chứng
definition_en: showing or producing the signs and symptoms of a disease or condition
example_sentence: Symptomatic patients exhibited elevated temperature, persistent cough, and fatigue, requiring immediate medical intervention and diagnostic testing.

definition_vi: phục hồi chức năng, tái hòa nhập
definition_en: to restore someone to health or normal life through therapy and training after illness, injury, or imprisonment
example_sentence: The physiotherapy program was designed to rehabilitate stroke survivors and help them regain independence in daily living activities.

definition_vi: bệnh kèm theo, có hai bệnh cùng lúc
definition_en: the simultaneous presence of two or more diseases or conditions in the same patient
example_sentence: The presence of comorbidity, such as diabetes and hypertension, considerably complicates treatment decisions and increases hospitalization risk.

definition_vi: chống chỉ định, lý do không nên dùng thuốc
definition_en: a condition that makes a particular treatment inadvisable, usually because it may prove harmful to the patient
example_sentence: Pregnancy is a major contraindication for this medication, so alternative treatments must be considered for pregnant women.

definition_vi: dịch bệnh toàn cầu, đại dịch
definition_en: a disease outbreak occurring over a geographically extensive area, typically affecting a large proportion of the population
example_sentence: The COVID-19 pandemic fundamentally transformed global healthcare systems and public health policies across all nations worldwide.

definition_vi: chăm sóc giảm nhẹ, chăm sóc từ biệt
definition_en: medical care focused on relieving suffering and improving quality of life rather than curing the disease
example_sentence: Palliative care teams work collaboratively to manage pain, provide emotional support, and maintain dignity for terminally ill patients.

definition_vi: liên quan đến điều trị, có tác dụng chữa bệnh
definition_en: relating to the healing of diseases or the treatment of disorders; having a beneficial effect on the body or mind
example_sentence: The therapeutic benefits of regular swimming for arthritis patients include improved mobility, reduced pain, and enhanced cardiovascular fitness.

definition_vi: dịch tễ học, khoa học về bệnh dịch
definition_en: the branch of medicine dealing with the incidence, distribution, and control of disease in populations
example_sentence: Epidemiology provides crucial data for understanding disease transmission patterns and implementing effective public health prevention strategies.

definition_vi: hồi phục, bệnh tạm thuyên giảm
definition_en: a temporary recovery or improvement in the symptoms of a disease, especially cancer, without complete cure
example_sentence: After intensive chemotherapy, the cancer patient achieved remission, offering hope for long-term survival and improved quality of life.

definition_vi: thử nghiệm lâm sàng, thử nghiệm trên người bệnh
definition_en: a research study performed in human volunteers to evaluate medical interventions, drugs, or treatments before approval
example_sentence: Rigorous clinical trials are essential for establishing the efficacy and safety of new pharmaceutical drugs before regulatory approval.

definition_vi: thời gian hồi phục sức khỏe
definition_en: the gradual recovery of strength and health after an illness or medical procedure
example_sentence: During convalescence following surgery, patients require adequate rest, nutrition, and physical therapy to restore full functional capacity.

definition_vi: nguyên nhân bệnh, bệnh căn nguyên
definition_en: the cause or origin of a disease or condition; the study of the causes of diseases
example_sentence: Understanding the etiology of cardiovascular disease is essential for developing effective prevention strategies and personalized treatment approaches.

definition_vi: di căn (bệnh ung thư), lan rộng
definition_en: to spread from one part of the body to another through the bloodstream or lymphatic system, as cancer cells do
example_sentence: If malignant cells metastasize to distant organs, the cancer stage becomes more advanced and treatment options become significantly more challenging.

definition_vi: tác nhân gây bệnh (vi khuẩn, virus, nấm, ký sinh trùng)
definition_en: a microorganism or substance that causes disease
example_sentence: Researchers identified the pathogen responsible for the outbreak within weeks of the first confirmed case.

definition_vi: dễ bị (bệnh, tác động), có khả năng mắc
definition_en: easily affected by or likely to suffer from something
example_sentence: Elderly individuals are more susceptible to severe respiratory infections due to weakened immune systems.

definition_vi: tỷ lệ mắc bệnh mới trong một khoảng thời gian nhất định
definition_en: the number of new cases of a disease occurring in a population during a specific period
example_sentence: The incidence rate of type 2 diabetes has doubled over the past two decades in developed nations.

definition_vi: phản ứng viêm của cơ thể (sưng, đỏ, nóng, đau)
definition_en: a localized physical condition with heat, swelling, and often pain as a reaction to injury or infection
example_sentence: Chronic inflammation in the joints is a hallmark feature of rheumatoid arthritis and requires long-term management.

definition_vi: rối loạn chuyển hóa (rối loạn quá trình hóa học trong cơ thể)
definition_en: a condition affecting the body's chemical processes for converting food into energy
example_sentence: Obesity is often associated with multiple metabolic disorders including insulin resistance and dyslipidemia.

definition_vi: hiệu quả của vắc-xin trong việc ngăn ngừa bệnh
definition_en: the ability of a vaccine to prevent disease in vaccinated individuals under ideal conditions
example_sentence: Clinical trials demonstrated that the new vaccine had an efficacy rate exceeding 95% against the target strain.

definition_vi: khuynh hướng di truyền (có gen làm tăng nguy cơ mắc bệnh)
definition_en: an inherited tendency to develop a particular disease or condition
example_sentence: Individuals with a genetic predisposition to breast cancer should undergo regular screening and consider preventive measures.

definition_vi: tác dụng phụ (phản ứng không mong muốn từ thuốc)
definition_en: an unintended reaction to a medication or treatment
example_sentence: Patients should be informed about potential side effects before starting any new medication regimen.

definition_vi: lối sống ít vận động, thiếu hoạt động thể chất
definition_en: a way of life involving little physical activity or exercise
example_sentence: A sedentary lifestyle is a major risk factor for cardiovascular disease, obesity, and metabolic syndrome.

definition_vi: thiếu hụt chất dinh dưỡng (không đủ vitamin, khoáng chất)
definition_en: insufficient intake or absorption of essential vitamins and minerals
example_sentence: Iron deficiency and vitamin B12 deficiency are common nutrient deficiencies affecting cognitive development in children.

definition_vi: tình trạng khủng hoảng sức khỏe tâm thần (cấp tính, nguy hiểm)
definition_en: an acute situation involving severe psychological distress or risk of self-harm
example_sentence: Healthcare systems must develop rapid response protocols for mental health crises to prevent tragic outcomes.

definition_vi: phương pháp toàn diện (xem xét cả thể chất, tâm lý, xã hội)
definition_en: a treatment method considering the whole person rather than just isolated symptoms
example_sentence: Modern medicine increasingly adopts a holistic approach that integrates physical, mental, and social dimensions of health.

definition_vi: chỉ dấu sinh học (chất chỉ thị bệnh trong máu, mô)
definition_en: a measurable biological indicator of disease or health status
example_sentence: Recent research identified novel biomarkers that could enable earlier detection of Alzheimer's disease before symptom onset.

definition_vi: y học phòng ngừa (tập trung vào ngăn chặn bệnh)
definition_en: medical practice focused on preventing disease rather than treating it after onset
example_sentence: Preventive medicine emphasizes vaccination, screening, and lifestyle modifications to reduce disease burden.

definition_vi: kháng thuốc kháng sinh (vi khuẩn không bị tiêu diệt bởi thuốc)
definition_en: the ability of microorganisms to resist the effects of antimicrobial drugs
example_sentence: Antimicrobial resistance poses a global health threat, with some bacteria now resistant to multiple classes of antibiotics.

definition_vi: suy giảm nhận thức (giảm khả năng tư duy, trí nhớ)
definition_en: a gradual loss of mental abilities such as memory, attention, and reasoning
synonyms: mental deterioration, loss of cognitive function
example_sentence: Early intervention with cognitive training and physical exercise may slow cognitive decline in aging populations.

definition_vi: nhiễm trùng đường hô hấp (cảm lạnh, viêm phế quản)
definition_en: an infection affecting the nose, throat, or lungs
synonyms: upper respiratory infection, lower respiratory infection
example_sentence: Respiratory tract infections are among the most common reasons for antibiotic prescription in primary care settings.

definition_vi: yếu tố nguy cơ tim mạch (huyết áp cao, cholesterol cao)
definition_en: a characteristic that increases the likelihood of developing heart or blood vessel disease
synonyms: cardiac risk factor, heart disease risk
example_sentence: Multiple cardiovascular risk factors including hypertension, diabetes, and smoking significantly increase mortality risk.

definition_vi: kết quả lâm sàng (kết quả điều trị, tiên lượng bệnh)
definition_en: the result or effect of medical treatment or intervention on a patient's health status
example_sentence: Researchers analyzed clinical outcomes across multiple hospitals to identify best practices for sepsis management.

definition_vi: ghép tạng (chuyển tạng từ người này sang người khác)
definition_en: a surgical procedure to transfer an organ from a donor to a recipient
example_sentence: Organ transplant recipients must take immunosuppressive medications for life to prevent rejection of the donor organ.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: nền văn minh, xã hội có trình độ phát triển cao
definition_en: the stage of human social development and organization that is considered most advanced
example_sentence: The ancient Egyptian civilization developed sophisticated systems of agriculture and governance that influenced Mediterranean societies for millennia.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: di sản, tài sản được thừa kế từ các thế hệ trước
definition_en: valuable objects and qualities that have belonged to the same family, country, or organization for many years
example_sentence: UNESCO recognizes Hoi An's architectural heritage as an outstanding example of Southeast Asian cultural exchange and preservation.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: nhà khảo cổ học, nhà khoa học nghiên cứu lịch sử qua các di vật
definition_en: a scientist who studies human history and prehistory through the examination of material remains
example_sentence: Archaeologists discovered evidence of early human settlements in the Mekong Delta, suggesting civilization flourished there over ten thousand years ago.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: di vật, đồ vật cổ xưa được bảo quản
definition_en: an object made by humans, typically an item of cultural or historical interest
example_sentence: The museum's collection includes pottery artifacts from the Bronze Age that reveal insights into daily life and trade practices of ancient peoples.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: triều đại, dòng dõi các vua chế độ thống trị liên tiếp
definition_en: a series of rulers belonging to the same family, or the period during which they rule
example_sentence: The Ming Dynasty's architectural achievements, particularly the Forbidden City and the Great Wall renovations, exemplified the cultural sophistication of Chinese civilization.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: thời đại, khoảng thời gian lịch sử đặc biệt
definition_en: a long and distinct period of history
example_sentence: The Renaissance era marked a transformative period when European scholars rediscovered classical texts and fundamentally reshaped intellectual and artistic traditions.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: sự khai quật, hoạt động đào bới tìm kiếm di vật
definition_en: the action or process of carefully digging out and removing something from the ground
example_sentence: Recent excavations at Angkor Wat have uncovered sophisticated water management systems that demonstrate the engineering prowess of the Khmer civilization.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: phát triển, thịnh vượng, lên cao
definition_en: to grow or develop in a healthy, vigorous way; to be very successful or productive
example_sentence: Trade routes through Central Asia allowed Silk Road civilizations to flourish economically and culturally, facilitating unprecedented exchange of goods and ideas.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: tượng đài, công trình kiến trúc để tưởng nhớ
definition_en: a statue, building, or other structure erected to commemorate a notable person or event
example_sentence: The pyramids of Giza represent monumental achievements of engineering that continue to astonish modern archaeologists and historians studying ancient Egyptian civilization.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: bảo tồn, giữ gìn, bảo vệ từ sự hủy hoại
definition_en: to maintain or keep something in its original or existing state; to protect from decay
example_sentence: International organizations work collectively to preserve important historical sites and cultural treasures for future generations of scholars and citizens worldwide.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: cổ xưa, thuộc thời kỳ lịch sử xa xưa
definition_en: belonging to the very distant past; of great age or very old
example_sentence: Ancient Greek philosophers developed foundational concepts in logic, ethics, and metaphysics that profoundly influenced Western intellectual traditions for over two millennia.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: có nguồn gốc từ, bắt đầu từ
definition_en: to find the origin or source of something; to discover the beginning point
example_sentence: Modern democracy can be traced back to the Athenian city-state, where citizens participated directly in governmental decision-making processes and civic responsibilities.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: thuộc thời Trung cổ, thời kỳ lịch sử giữa Cổ đại và Hiện đại
definition_en: relating to the Middle Ages, the period of European history from about the fifth to fifteenth centuries
example_sentence: Medieval European castles served not merely as military fortifications but also as administrative centers where feudal lords governed vast territories and peasant populations.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: suy thoái, sụt giảm, hạ xuống
definition_en: to become smaller, fewer, or less; to grow weaker or fall into worse condition
example_sentence: The Roman Empire's decline resulted from multiple interconnected factors including military overexpansion, economic instability, and political corruption throughout its vast territories.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: cuộc nổi dậy, cuộc khởi nghĩa chống lại quyền lực
definition_en: a rebellion or revolt against authority or an established government
example_sentence: The peasant uprisings throughout medieval Europe challenged feudal hierarchies and demanded greater social and economic rights from their noble overlords.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: sự chinh phục, cuộc chiếm đoạt lãnh thổ bằng vũ lực
definition_en: the subjugation and assumption of control of a place or people by military force
example_sentence: The Norman Conquest of England in 1066 profoundly transformed English society, introducing French feudal systems and fundamentally altering the linguistic composition of the realm.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: di sản, khoản thừa kế, ảnh hưởng còn lại từ quá khứ
definition_en: something handed down or remaining from a person or thing of the past; a lasting impact or influence
example_sentence: The legacy of Islamic Golden Age scholarship fundamentally shaped mathematics, astronomy, and medicine, establishing scientific methodologies that remained authoritative for centuries.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: bản địa, sơ khai, có nguồn gốc từ một vùng nào đó
definition_en: originating or occurring naturally in a particular place; native
example_sentence: Indigenous peoples of the Americas developed sophisticated agricultural techniques, including the domestication of maize, potatoes, and quinoa that sustain global populations today.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: ghi chép, lập sử ký, kể lại sự kiện theo thứ tự thời gian
definition_en: to record events in the order they happened; to describe or narrate historical events
example_sentence: Medieval monks meticulously chronicled historical events, religious teachings, and daily monastic life in illuminated manuscripts that preserved crucial knowledge for subsequent generations.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: sự khôi phục, việc sửa chữa trả lại tình trạng cũ
definition_en: the action or process of returning something to a former, original condition; the reconstruction of something damaged
example_sentence: The restoration of the Angkor Wat temple complex involved international cooperation and sophisticated conservation techniques to preserve its intricate stone carvings and architectural integrity.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: thời kỳ lịch sử có đặc điểm riêng biệt; kỷ nguyên
definition_en: a period of time in history or a person's life, typically one marked by notable events or characteristics
example_sentence: The Renaissance epoch marked a dramatic shift from medieval to modern thinking in European culture.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: do con người gây ra; liên quan đến hoạt động của loài người
definition_en: caused or produced by human activities, especially referring to environmental change
example_sentence: Anthropogenic climate change has reshaped civilizations' settlement patterns throughout the Holocene epoch.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: liên quan đến chính trị dựa trên vị trí địa lý; địa chính trị
definition_en: relating to politics and geography, especially the influence of geography on international relations and power
example_sentence: The geopolitical significance of the Silk Road determined which empires would dominate trade and cultural exchange.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: sự tồn tại; cách sống tự cấp; kinh tế tự túc
definition_en: the minimum level of income or resources needed to maintain life; a way of living at bare survival level
example_sentence: Early agricultural societies transitioned from hunter-gatherer subsistence to settled farming communities.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: sự phân tầng; sự chia thành các lớp xã hội
definition_en: the arrangement of society into distinct layers or classes; the process of dividing into strata
example_sentence: Social stratification in ancient Egypt created rigid class divisions between pharaohs, nobles, and peasants.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: nước ngoài phải nộp thuế/tiền cho nước khác; phụ thuộc
definition_en: a state or region that pays taxes or tribute to a more powerful state; subordinate
example_sentence: Many Asian kingdoms became tributaries of the Chinese empire, maintaining political autonomy while acknowledging imperial supremacy.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: sự tản cư; cộng đồng người cùng dân tộc sống rải rác ở nước ngoài
definition_en: the dispersion of a people from their original homeland; communities of such dispersed people
example_sentence: The Jewish diaspora following the Roman destruction of Jerusalem profoundly influenced Mediterranean and Middle Eastern societies.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: quyền thống trị; sự thống lĩnh của một quốc gia/nhóm
definition_en: the dominance of one social group, nation, or culture over others; leadership or supremacy
example_sentence: British hegemony in the 19th century extended across continents through colonial expansion and naval superiority.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: kết hợp các yếu tố từ nhiều nguồn khác nhau; pha trộn
definition_en: combining or reconciling different beliefs, practices, or traditions into a unified system
example_sentence: Syncretic religions in Latin America blended indigenous spiritual practices with Catholic Christianity during the colonial period.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: liên quan đến nông nghiệp; người ủng hộ cải cách đất đai
definition_en: relating to agriculture or farmers; an advocate for agricultural interests or land reform
example_sentence: The agrarian revolution of the 18th century transformed European economies through improved farming techniques and crop rotation.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: chủ nghĩa thương mại; hệ thống kinh tế coi xuất khẩu là quan trọng
definition_en: an economic system emphasizing national wealth accumulation through trade surplus and colonial expansion
example_sentence: European mercantilism drove colonial competition in the Americas as nations sought to maximize precious metal exports.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: quá trình tách rời tôn giáo khỏi chính trị/xã hội; thế tục hóa
definition_en: the process of removing religious influence from government, education, and society
example_sentence: The Enlightenment accelerated secularization in Europe, challenging religious authority and promoting rational thought.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: du mục; không có nơi cư trú cố định
definition_en: relating to people who move from place to place rather than settling permanently in one location
example_sentence: Nomadic pastoral societies on the Central Asian steppes developed sophisticated trade networks connecting East and West.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: chế độ phong kiến; hệ thống xã hội dựa trên mối quan hệ chủ-tôi
definition_en: a political and economic system based on the exchange of land for military service and loyalty
example_sentence: European feudalism created hierarchical social structures where vassals pledged allegiance to lords in exchange for land protection.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: sự thay đổi cơ bản trong cách suy nghĩ/hiểu biết; chuyển mô hình
definition_en: a fundamental change in the basic concepts and practices of a discipline or society
example_sentence: The Scientific Revolution represented a paradigm shift from religious authority to empirical observation in understanding the natural world.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: liên quan đến cả xã hội lẫn chính trị; xã hội-chính trị
definition_en: relating to both social and political factors or movements
example_sentence: The French Revolution was driven by complex sociopolitical tensions between the aristocracy, bourgeoisie, and peasantry.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: cơ sở hạ tầng; các công trình cơ bản của một xã hội
definition_en: the basic physical systems and facilities needed for a society or civilization to function
example_sentence: Roman infrastructure, including aqueducts and roads, enabled the empire to maintain control over vast territories.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: thuộc về thế giới; không bị giới hạn bởi quốc gia; người thế giới
definition_en: familiar with many different countries and cultures; a person with such broad experience
example_sentence: Alexandria in Hellenistic Egypt became a cosmopolitan center where Greek, Egyptian, and Jewish cultures interacted.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: liên quan đến dân số; nhân khẩu học
definition_en: relating to the statistical study of human populations, including size, growth, and composition
example_sentence: Demographic changes in medieval Europe, including plague mortality, fundamentally altered social and economic structures.

set_name: Lịch sử & Văn minh nhân loại
definition_vi: liên quan đến tư tưởng; ý thức hệ
definition_en: relating to a system of ideas and beliefs that characterizes a group, movement, or society
example_sentence: Ideological conflicts between communist and capitalist powers shaped geopolitical tensions throughout the 20th century.

definition_vi: cải thiện, nâng cao chất lượng hoặc giá trị của cái gì
definition_en: to improve the quality, value, or attractiveness of something
example_sentence: The new technology will enhance productivity and reduce operational costs significantly.

definition_vi: giảm bớt, làm cho nhỏ lại hoặc yếu đi
definition_en: to make or become smaller or less in size, amount, or importance
example_sentence: Pollution continues to diminish air quality in urban areas across the country.

definition_vi: dồi dào, có nhiều, phong phú
definition_en: existing or available in large quantities; plentiful
example_sentence: The region has abundant natural resources that support its growing economy.

definition_vi: hiếm, khan hiếm, không đủ
definition_en: not enough or available in only small quantities
example_sentence: Fresh water is becoming increasingly scarce in many developing nations.

definition_vi: tạo điều kiện, giúp dễ dàng hơn, thúc đẩy
definition_en: to make something easier or help it happen more smoothly
example_sentence: Digital platforms facilitate communication between researchers worldwide.

definition_vi: cản trở, gây khó khăn, làm chậm lại
definition_en: to create difficulties that prevent or slow down progress
example_sentence: Lack of funding hindered the research team's ability to complete the project.

definition_vi: thúc đẩy, khuyến khích, nâng cao vị trí
definition_en: to encourage or support the development or popularity of something
example_sentence: The government promotes sustainable practices to protect the environment.

definition_vi: đàn áp, che giấu, ngăn chặn
definition_en: to prevent something from being known, expressed, or developed
example_sentence: The regime attempted to suppress freedom of speech and media.

definition_vi: rõ ràng, minh bạch, không ẩn chứa
definition_en: stated clearly and in detail, leaving no room for confusion
example_sentence: The contract contains explicit instructions regarding payment terms and conditions.

definition_vi: ẩn chứa, không nói rõ nhưng được hiểu
definition_en: suggested or understood without being directly stated
example_sentence: There is an implicit agreement between the parties regarding confidentiality.

definition_vi: dễ vỡ, yếu ớt, không bền vững
definition_en: easily broken, damaged, or destroyed; delicate
example_sentence: The ecosystem is fragile and requires careful protection from human activities.

definition_vi: vững chắc, bền bỉ, mạnh mẽ
definition_en: strong, solid, and able to withstand pressure or difficult conditions
example_sentence: The new framework provides a robust foundation for sustainable development.

definition_vi: tăng tốc, đẩy nhanh, làm nhanh lên
definition_en: to increase in speed or cause something to happen faster
example_sentence: Climate change is accelerating the melting of polar ice caps.

definition_vi: giảm tốc, làm chậm lại
definition_en: to reduce in speed or slow down
example_sentence: Economic growth is expected to decelerate in the coming quarters.

definition_vi: mở rộng, phát triển, tăng kích thước
definition_en: to increase in size, volume, or scope
example_sentence: The company plans to expand its operations into three new markets.

definition_vi: co lại, giảm kích thước, rút gọn
definition_en: to become smaller or reduce in size, volume, or scope
example_sentence: The manufacturing sector contracted significantly during the economic recession.

definition_vi: minh bạch, rõ ràng, không che giấu
definition_en: open and honest; allowing information to be seen or understood clearly
example_sentence: Transparent governance practices build public trust in institutions.

definition_vi: mờ ám, không rõ ràng, che giấu
definition_en: not clear or transparent; difficult to understand or see through
example_sentence: The company's opaque financial reporting raised concerns among investors.

definition_vi: củng cố, gộp lại, làm chắc chắn
definition_en: to combine or strengthen separate elements into a unified whole
example_sentence: The merger will consolidate the company's position as market leader.

definition_vi: phân tán, rải rác, tán ra
definition_en: to spread out or scatter in different directions
example_sentence: Seeds are dispersed by wind and animals across vast distances.

definition_vi: duy trì, hỗ trợ, giữ vững
definition_en: to maintain or support something over a long period
example_sentence: Renewable energy sources can sustain economic growth without harming the environment.

definition_vi: bỏ rơi, từ bỏ, thôi không tiếp tục
definition_en: to leave or give up something completely, often without intention to return
example_sentence: The project was abandoned due to insufficient funding and resources.

definition_vi: làm giảm bớt mức độ nghiêm trọng hoặc tác hại của cái gì; giảm nhẹ
definition_en: to make something less severe or serious; to reduce the impact of something negative
example_sentence: The government implemented new policies to mitigate the effects of climate change on coastal communities.

definition_vi: làm tình huống trở nên tệ hơn; làm trầm trọng thêm
definition_en: to make a problem or situation worse; to intensify negative conditions
example_sentence: Poor air quality exacerbates respiratory diseases in urban areas, particularly affecting vulnerable populations.

definition_vi: có thể hiểu theo nhiều cách khác nhau; không rõ ràng, mơ hồ
definition_en: open to more than one interpretation; unclear or uncertain in meaning
example_sentence: The contract contained ambiguous language that led to disputes between the two parties.

definition_vi: thực tế, thiết thực; dựa trên những gì có thể làm được thay vì lý tưởng
definition_en: dealing with things in a practical, realistic way based on actual circumstances rather than theory
example_sentence: The company took a pragmatic approach to reducing costs by focusing on operational efficiency rather than layoffs.

definition_vi: dễ thay đổi, không ổn định; (về người) dễ nổi nóng, bất ổn định về cảm xúc
definition_en: liable to change rapidly and unpredictably; unstable or prone to sudden emotional outbursts
example_sentence: Stock markets became increasingly volatile following the announcement of unexpected economic data.

definition_vi: logic, mạch lạc; (về người) sáng suốt, có khả năng diễn đạt rõ ràng
definition_en: logical and consistent; able to be understood clearly; making sense as a whole
example_sentence: The researcher presented a coherent argument supported by extensive empirical evidence.

definition_vi: nông cạn, không sâu sắc; chỉ ở bề mặt, không đi sâu vào bản chất
definition_en: lacking depth; concerned only with what is obvious or apparent; not thorough or genuine
example_sentence: The media coverage of the scandal was criticized for being superficial and failing to investigate underlying causes.

definition_vi: tỉ mỉ, cẩn thận; chú ý đến từng chi tiết nhỏ nhất
definition_en: showing great attention to detail; very careful and precise in work or behavior
example_sentence: The auditor conducted a meticulous examination of the company's financial records.

definition_vi: mâu thuẫn, điều tưởng chừng như vô lý nhưng lại có thể đúng
definition_en: a situation or statement that seems contradictory but may reveal a deeper truth
example_sentence: The paradox of modern technology is that it connects people globally while often isolating them locally.

definition_vi: tìm cách vòng qua, né tránh; vượt qua một cách khéo léo
definition_en: to find a way around an obstacle or rule; to avoid or bypass something
example_sentence: Some companies attempt to circumvent environmental regulations by relocating to countries with weaker standards.

definition_vi: vốn có, bẩm sinh; là một phần tự nhiên của cái gì
definition_en: existing as a natural, permanent, or inseparable element or quality
example_sentence: There are inherent risks in any investment strategy that investors must carefully consider.

definition_vi: tùy tiện, không có cơ sở lý do; được quyết định bởi cá nhân chứ không phải quy tắc
definition_en: based on random choice or personal whim rather than reason or system; not following a logical principle
example_sentence: Critics argued that the selection process was arbitrary and lacked transparent criteria.

definition_vi: chứng minh là đúng; bảo vệ danh dự, làm sạch tên tuổi
definition_en: to prove or justify that someone or something is right or correct; to clear from blame
example_sentence: Recent scientific findings vindicated the researcher's controversial hypothesis from a decade earlier.

definition_vi: tự mãn, thỏa mãn với hiện tại; không lo lắng hoặc cố gắng cải thiện
definition_en: satisfied with oneself or one's achievements; lacking concern or motivation for improvement
example_sentence: The company became complacent after years of market dominance, failing to innovate and losing ground to competitors.

definition_vi: đặt cạnh nhau để so sánh hoặc tạo hiệu ứng tương phản
definition_en: to place two things side by side, especially for comparison or to create an interesting effect
example_sentence: The artist juxtaposed images of nature and industrial development to highlight environmental concerns.

definition_vi: xác nhận, chứng thực; cung cấp bằng chứng hỗ trợ cho một tuyên bố
definition_en: to confirm or give support to a statement, theory, or finding with additional evidence
example_sentence: Multiple witnesses corroborated the defendant's account of events on the night in question.

definition_vi: có thể cảm nhận được, hữu hình; rõ ràng, cụ thể (không trừu tượng)
definition_en: able to be touched or felt; real and concrete rather than abstract or imaginary
example_sentence: The project delivered tangible results within the first quarter, including a 25% increase in productivity.

definition_vi: dựa trên suy đoán, không chắc chắn; liên quan đến việc đầu cơ hoặc mạo hiểm
definition_en: based on conjecture or guesswork rather than facts; involving risk or uncertainty
example_sentence: The economist warned against speculative investments in emerging markets without proper due diligence.

definition_vi: giáo dục; quá trình học tập và phát triển kỹ năng
definition_en: the process of teaching and learning, especially in schools
example_sentence: Quality education is essential for developing critical thinking skills in young people.

definition_vi: thành tích; điều đạt được sau nỗ lực
definition_en: a successful accomplishment or attainment of a goal
example_sentence: Her academic achievement in mathematics earned her a scholarship to university.

definition_vi: sự phát triển; quá trình tăng trưởng hoặc cải tiến
definition_en: the process of growing, improving, or becoming more advanced
example_sentence: Sustainable development requires balancing economic growth with environmental protection.

definition_vi: trách nhiệm; bổn phận hoặc nghĩa vụ phải thực hiện
definition_en: the state of being accountable for something or someone
example_sentence: Students have a responsibility to complete their assignments on time and maintain academic integrity.

definition_vi: sáng tạo; khả năng tạo ra những ý tưởng mới
definition_en: the ability to produce original ideas or artistic work
example_sentence: The project encourages creativity by allowing students to design their own solutions.

definition_vi: năng suất; mức độ hiệu quả trong công việc
definition_en: the effectiveness of effort measured by the rate of output per unit of input
example_sentence: Regular breaks actually improve workplace productivity by reducing mental fatigue.

definition_vi: liên quan đến môi trường; thuộc về tự nhiên và sinh thái
definition_en: relating to the natural world and the impact of human activity on it
example_sentence: Environmental protection has become a critical issue in international policy discussions.

definition_vi: cảm xúc; liên quan đến cảm giác và tâm trạng
definition_en: relating to feelings or the expression of feelings
example_sentence: Emotional intelligence is increasingly recognized as important as academic knowledge in education.

definition_vi: thực tế; có thể áp dụng vào thực tiễn
definition_en: concerned with actual use or practice rather than theory
example_sentence: The course combines theoretical knowledge with practical experience in laboratory settings.

definition_vi: phê phán; có tính chất đánh giá hoặc rất quan trọng
definition_en: expressing disapproval or involving careful judgment; essential or crucial
example_sentence: Critical thinking skills enable students to analyze information and make informed decisions.

definition_vi: hạnh phúc; trạng thái vui vẻ và hài lòng
definition_en: the state of being happy or content
example_sentence: Research shows that meaningful relationships contribute significantly to long-term happiness.

definition_vi: nhận thức; sự hiểu biết hoặc chú ý đến điều gì
definition_en: knowledge or perception of a situation or fact
example_sentence: Public awareness campaigns have successfully reduced plastic consumption in many communities.

definition_vi: cải tiến; sự làm tốt hơn hoặc phát triển
definition_en: the process of making something better or the result of making something better
example_sentence: Continuous improvement in teaching methods has led to better student outcomes.

definition_vi: bảo vệ; hành động hoặc biện pháp che chở khỏi nguy hiểm
definition_en: the action of protecting someone or something from harm or injury
example_sentence: Data protection regulations require companies to secure personal information responsibly.

definition_vi: giao tiếp; quá trình trao đổi thông tin giữa các bên
definition_en: the imparting or exchanging of information or news
example_sentence: Effective communication between teachers and parents improves student academic performance.

definition_vi: một cách tích cực; với sự tham gia hoặc nỗ lực
definition_en: in an engaged or energetic manner; with participation
example_sentence: Students who actively participate in class discussions tend to retain information better.

definition_vi: đáng kể; một cách có ý nghĩa hoặc tác động lớn
definition_en: in a sufficiently great or important way to be worthy of attention
example_sentence: Climate change has significantly affected agricultural production in developing countries.

definition_vi: dần dần; từ từ theo thời gian
definition_en: in a slow or gradual manner; by degrees
example_sentence: Language skills develop gradually through consistent practice and exposure.

definition_vi: giáo dục; liên quan đến việc dạy và học
definition_en: relating to the process of teaching and learning
example_sentence: Educational technology has transformed how students access and engage with learning materials.

definition_vi: điều trị; cách xử lý hoặc chăm sóc ai/cái gì
definition_en: the action or manner of dealing with something; medical care for a disease
example_sentence: Early treatment of mental health issues can prevent more serious complications later.

definition_vi: đầu tư; tiền hoặc tài nguyên dành cho mục đích sinh lợi
definition_en: the action of investing money for profit; something worth spending time or effort on
example_sentence: Investment in renewable energy is crucial for sustainable economic development.

definition_vi: đa dạng; sự khác nhau về loại, chủng loại hoặc đặc điểm
definition_en: the state of being diverse; variety in terms of race, gender, or background
example_sentence: Cultural diversity in the classroom enriches learning experiences for all students.

definition_vi: cạnh tranh; liên quan đến sự so sánh hoặc đua đòi
definition_en: relating to or characterized by competition; striving to win or be better than others
example_sentence: The competitive job market requires graduates to develop specialized skills and experience.

definition_vi: sự thành lập, thiết lập; tổ chức, cơ quan; lớp người có quyền lực trong xã hội
definition_en: the act of founding or setting up; an institution or organization; the existing power structure
example_sentence: The establishment of new environmental regulations has significantly impacted industrial practices across the nation.

definition_vi: sự khuyến khích, động viên; điều gì đó làm tăng cơ hội thành công
definition_en: the action of giving support or confidence; something that promotes or stimulates action
example_sentence: With proper encouragement from teachers, students are more likely to pursue advanced studies in STEM fields.

definition_vi: sự điều chỉnh, thích nghi; sự thay đổi nhỏ để phù hợp
definition_en: a small change made to correct or improve something; adaptation to new conditions
example_sentence: The adjustment of school curricula to include digital literacy has become essential in modern education.

definition_vi: sự thực thi, thi hành (luật lệ); sự ép buộc tuân theo
definition_en: the act of compelling compliance with a law or rule; implementation of regulations
example_sentence: Strict enforcement of traffic laws has reduced accident rates in urban areas significantly.

definition_vi: sự tham gia, liên quan; mối quan hệ lâu dài; sự hứa hẹn
definition_en: the state of being involved or committed; a promise to marry; active participation
example_sentence: Student engagement in extracurricular activities correlates strongly with improved academic performance.

definition_vi: sự mở rộng, phóng to; sự tăng kích thước
definition_en: the action of making something larger; an increase in size or scope
example_sentence: The enlargement of the university's research facilities has attracted leading scholars from around the world.

definition_vi: sự sắp xếp, bố trí; thỏa thuận, thoả thuận; cách sắp xếp
definition_en: the action of organizing or putting in order; a plan or agreement made in advance
example_sentence: The arrangement of classroom seating can influence student collaboration and peer learning outcomes.

definition_vi: sự thay thế, thay chỗ; người/vật thay thế
definition_en: a person or thing that takes the place of another; the action of substituting
example_sentence: The replacement of outdated textbooks with digital resources has modernized the learning experience.

definition_vi: sự đánh giá, định giá; kết quả đánh giá; bài kiểm tra
definition_en: the evaluation or estimation of the nature, ability, or quality of something; a test or examination
example_sentence: Continuous assessment throughout the semester provides more accurate feedback than a single final examination.

definition_vi: sự quản lý, điều hành; nhóm người quản lý; cách xử lý
definition_en: the process of controlling and organizing business operations; the people responsible for running an organization
example_sentence: Effective time management is crucial for students balancing academic work with extracurricular activities.

definition_vi: sự tiến bộ, phát triển; sự thăng chức; tiền cho vay trước
definition_en: progress or improvement; promotion to a higher position; a loan given before it is due
example_sentence: Career advancement in the technology sector often requires continuous learning and skill development.

definition_vi: yêu cầu, điều kiện cần thiết; nhu cầu, sự cần thiết
definition_en: something needed or demanded; a condition that must be satisfied
example_sentence: University entrance requirements typically include standardized test scores and a minimum GPA.

definition_vi: khoảng cách thành tích học tập giữa các nhóm học sinh khác nhau
definition_en: the difference in academic performance between groups of students, often based on socioeconomic status or ethnicity
example_sentence: Addressing the achievement gap requires targeted interventions and equitable resource allocation in schools.

definition_vi: quá trình học và phát triển kỹ năng; sự có được kỹ năng
definition_en: the process of learning and developing competencies through practice and experience
example_sentence: Skill acquisition in language learning accelerates when students engage in immersive and interactive environments.

definition_vi: sự hòa nhập xã hội; quá trình các nhóm khác nhau hợp nhất
definition_en: the process of individuals or groups becoming part of a unified society; inclusion in social structures
example_sentence: Social integration programs help immigrant students adapt to new school environments and build meaningful friendships.

definition_vi: khung cơ chế tổ chức; hệ thống các quy tắc và cấu trúc của tổ chức
definition_en: the system of rules, structures, and procedures that govern an organization or institution
example_sentence: The institutional framework of universities ensures quality assurance and accountability in academic programs.

definition_vi: kết quả có thể đo lường được; những thành quả cụ thể có thể định lượng
definition_en: specific, quantifiable results that can be assessed and evaluated objectively
example_sentence: Educational programs should establish clear measurable outcomes to demonstrate their effectiveness and impact.

definition_vi: Vật thể trên trời (sao, hành tinh, vệ tinh, v.v.)
definition_en: Any natural object existing in space, such as a star, planet, or satellite
example_sentence: Astronomers discovered a new celestial body orbiting within our solar system using advanced telescope technology.

definition_vi: Quỹ đạo; chuyển động quanh một vật thể theo quỹ đạo
definition_en: The curved path of a celestial object around another object in space; to move in such a path
example_sentence: The Moon orbits the Earth approximately every 27 days, maintaining a nearly circular orbital trajectory throughout its revolution.

definition_vi: Thiên hà; tập hợp lớn những ngôi sao
definition_en: A massive system of stars, stellar remnants, and interstellar matter bound together by gravity
example_sentence: The Milky Way galaxy contains an estimated 200 billion stars distributed across its vast spiral structure.

definition_vi: Lực hấp dẫn; sức kéo của trọng lực
definition_en: The attractive force exerted by a massive object on other objects in space
example_sentence: The Sun's gravitational pull keeps all planets in our solar system in stable orbits around its massive core.

definition_vi: Liên quan đến sao; xuất sắc
definition_en: Relating to stars; or of outstanding quality and excellence
example_sentence: The stellar composition of distant stars reveals crucial information about the chemical elements formed during cosmic nucleosynthesis.

definition_vi: Bầu khí quyển; lớp khí bao quanh một hành tinh
definition_en: The layer of gases surrounding a planet, star, or other celestial body
example_sentence: Mars's thin atmosphere, composed primarily of carbon dioxide, cannot support human life without protective equipment.

definition_vi: Bức xạ vũ trụ; các hạt năng lượng cao từ vũ trụ
definition_en: High-energy particles and electromagnetic radiation originating from outer space
example_sentence: Astronauts aboard the International Space Station are exposed to increased cosmic radiation compared to people living on Earth's surface.

definition_vi: Nhật thực hoặc nguyệt thực; sự che phủ
definition_en: An astronomical event where one celestial body partially or completely blocks another from view
example_sentence: During the total solar eclipse, the Moon completely eclipsed the Sun, casting Earth into temporary darkness at midday.

definition_vi: Tinh vân; đám mây khí và bụi giữa các ngôi sao
definition_en: A cloud of gas and dust in space that may eventually form new stars
example_sentence: The Orion Nebula is an emission nebula where new stars are actively forming within dense regions of interstellar gas.

definition_vi: Lỗ đen; vùng không gian có trọng lực cực mạnh
definition_en: A region of spacetime where gravity is so strong that nothing can escape from it, not even light
example_sentence: Scientists have detected gravitational waves from the collision of two black holes located over one billion light-years away.

definition_vi: Tàu vũ trụ; phương tiện bay vào không gian
definition_en: A vehicle designed for traveling in space beyond Earth's atmosphere
example_sentence: The Voyager spacecraft have traveled beyond the solar system, carrying messages from humanity to potential extraterrestrial civilizations.

definition_vi: Chòm sao; nhóm các ngôi sao
definition_en: A recognizable pattern of stars visible from Earth, traditionally identified with mythological figures
example_sentence: The constellation Orion is one of the most recognizable star patterns visible during winter months in the Northern Hemisphere.

definition_vi: Phát ra; tỏa ra (ánh sáng, năng lượng)
definition_en: To discharge or release something, such as light, heat, or radiation
example_sentence: Pulsars emit powerful beams of radio waves and X-rays as they rotate rapidly in space at incredible speeds.

definition_vi: Tàu thăm dò vũ trụ; điều tra, khảo sát
definition_en: An unmanned spacecraft designed to gather scientific information; to investigate or examine closely
example_sentence: NASA's Cassini probe explored Saturn's rings for over thirteen years, providing unprecedented data about the planet's structure.

definition_vi: Liên quan đến Mặt Trăng
definition_en: Relating to or characteristic of the Moon
example_sentence: The lunar surface is covered with craters formed by meteorite impacts over billions of years of cosmic bombardment.

definition_vi: Liên quan đến Trái Đất
definition_en: Relating to the Earth or land; existing on land rather than in water or space
example_sentence: Terrestrial planets like Earth and Mars are composed primarily of rock and metal, unlike gaseous giant planets.

definition_vi: Phóng (tàu vũ trụ); sự phóng
definition_en: To send a spacecraft into space; the act of starting a space mission
example_sentence: SpaceX successfully launched the Falcon 9 rocket carrying international astronauts to the International Space Station.

definition_vi: Năm ánh sáng; khoảng cách ánh sáng đi trong một năm
definition_en: The distance that light travels in a vacuum in one year; a unit of astronomical distance measurement
example_sentence: The nearest star system to Earth, Alpha Centauri, is located approximately 4.37 light-years away from our solar system.

definition_vi: Ngoài Trái Đất; sinh vật ngoài Trái Đất
definition_en: Existing or originating outside the Earth or its atmosphere; a being from outer space
example_sentence: Scientists continue searching for evidence of extraterrestrial life through the SETI program by analyzing radio signals from distant galaxies.

definition_vi: Lĩnh vực vật lý học nghiên cứu chuyển động của các vật thể trên bầu trời dựa trên các định luật Newton và lực hấp dẫn.
definition_en: The branch of physics dealing with the motion and gravitational effects of celestial objects.
example_sentence: Celestial mechanics enables scientists to predict planetary positions and satellite trajectories with remarkable precision.

definition_vi: Đĩa vật chất xoay quanh một vật thể có khối lượng lớn (như sao neutron hay lỗ đen), nơi vật chất rơi vào và phát sáng mạnh.
definition_en: A rotating disk of material surrounding a massive object, where infalling matter releases energy as radiation.
example_sentence: The accretion disk around the supermassive black hole at the galaxy's center emits intense X-rays.

definition_vi: Hiện tượng ánh sáng từ các vật thể xa xôi bị dịch chuyển về phía đỏ do vật thể đó đang chuyển động ra xa hoặc vũ trụ đang giãn nở.
definition_en: The shift of light toward longer wavelengths due to the recession of distant objects or cosmic expansion.
example_sentence: Astronomers use redshift measurements to determine the recession velocity of distant galaxies.

definition_vi: Lớp bề ngoài của Mặt Trời hoặc một ngôi sao khác, nơi ánh sáng được phát ra và có thể quan sát được từ Trái Đất.
definition_en: The visible outer layer of a star from which light is emitted.
example_sentence: Sunspots appear as dark regions on the Sun's photosphere due to intense magnetic activity.

definition_vi: Lớp khí nóng nằm ngay phía trên quang cầu của Mặt Trời, có nhiệt độ cao hơn quang cầu.
definition_en: The layer of the Sun's atmosphere above the photosphere, characterized by rising temperature.
example_sentence: During a total solar eclipse, the chromosphere becomes visible as a thin reddish ring around the Sun.

definition_vi: Một vụ phát nổ mạnh mẽ trên bề mặt Mặt Trời, phát ra năng lượng khổng lồ dưới dạng ánh sáng, tia X và hạt.
definition_en: A sudden, intense burst of radiation from the Sun's surface, releasing enormous energy.
example_sentence: A powerful solar flare can disrupt satellite communications and power grids on Earth.

definition_vi: Sự phát thải khối lượng lớn plasma từ vương miện Mặt Trời ra ngoài không gian, thường liên quan đến hoạt động từ tính.
definition_en: A large expulsion of plasma from the Sun's corona into space, often associated with solar activity.
example_sentence: Coronal mass ejections can trigger geomagnetic storms that affect Earth's magnetosphere.

definition_vi: Sự thay đổi vị trí biểu kiến của một vật thể khi quan sát từ hai vị trí khác nhau, được sử dụng để đo khoảng cách đến các sao gần.
definition_en: The apparent shift in position of an object when viewed from two different locations, used to measure stellar distances.
example_sentence: Hipparcos satellite measurements of stellar parallax have revolutionized our understanding of cosmic distances.

definition_vi: Thang đo độ sáng của một ngôi sao hoặc thiên thể, với các giá trị nhỏ hơn biểu thị độ sáng lớn hơn.
definition_en: A measure of the brightness of a celestial object on a logarithmic scale.
example_sentence: The apparent magnitude of Venus can reach −4.7, making it the brightest planet visible from Earth.

definition_vi: Kỹ thuật phân tích ánh sáng từ các ngôi sao để xác định thành phần hóa học, nhiệt độ và chuyển động của chúng.
definition_en: The analysis of light from celestial objects to determine their composition, temperature, and motion.
example_sentence: Spectroscopy has revealed that distant galaxies contain the same chemical elements as our own.

definition_vi: Hiện tượng tần số sóng thay đổi khi nguồn phát và người quan sát chuyển động tương đối với nhau.
definition_en: The change in frequency of waves due to relative motion between source and observer.
example_sentence: The Doppler effect allows astronomers to measure the radial velocity of stars and galaxies.

definition_vi: Vùng quanh một ngôi sao nơi điều kiện cho phép nước lỏng tồn tại trên bề mặt hành tinh, có khả năng hỗ trợ sự sống.
definition_en: The region around a star where conditions permit liquid water to exist on a planet's surface.
example_sentence: Proxima Centauri b orbits within the habitable zone of its star, making it a candidate for potential life.

definition_vi: Một hành tinh quay quanh một ngôi sao khác ngoài Hệ Mặt Trời của chúng ta.
definition_en: A planet that orbits a star outside our solar system.
example_sentence: Over 5,000 exoplanets have been discovered to date, expanding our understanding of planetary diversity.

definition_vi: Kỹ thuật phát hiện hành tinh ngoài Hệ Mặt Trời bằng cách đo sự giảm độ sáng của ngôi sao khi hành tinh đi qua trước nó.
definition_en: A technique for detecting exoplanets by measuring the dimming of starlight as a planet passes in front of its star.
example_sentence: The transit method has been responsible for discovering the majority of known exoplanets.

definition_vi: Thành phần vận tốc của một vật thể theo hướng nhìn từ Trái Đất, được xác định bằng dịch chuyển Doppler.
definition_en: The component of an object's velocity along the line of sight, determined by Doppler shift.
example_sentence: Radial velocity measurements have enabled the detection of exoplanets around nearby stars.

definition_vi: Một vụ nổ sao cực kỳ mạnh mẽ, làm cho ngôi sao tạm thời trở nên sáng hơn cả một thiên hà.
definition_en: An extremely powerful stellar explosion that can briefly outshine an entire galaxy.
example_sentence: Type Ia supernovae are used as standard candles to measure cosmic distances.

definition_vi: Một ngôi sao suy biến cực kỳ đặc, được tạo thành chủ yếu từ neutron, với khối lượng rất lớn trong thể tích nhỏ.
definition_en: An extremely dense stellar remnant composed primarily of neutrons, with immense mass in a small volume.
example_sentence: A neutron star's density is so extreme that a teaspoon of its material would weigh billions of tons.

definition_vi: Một ngôi sao neutron quay nhanh phát ra chùm bức xạ điện từ định kỳ, tạo ra tín hiệu xung như một ngọn hải đăng.
definition_en: A rapidly rotating neutron star that emits periodic bursts of electromagnetic radiation.
example_sentence: The first pulsar discovered in 1967 was initially thought to be a signal from extraterrestrial intelligence.

definition_vi: Một ngôi sao suy biến nhỏ, đặc, là phần dư lại sau khi một ngôi sao giống Mặt Trời kết thúc cuộc đời của nó.
definition_en: A small, dense stellar remnant left behind after a Sun-like star exhausts its fuel.
example_sentence: Sirius B, the companion of Sirius, is a white dwarf with a mass comparable to the Sun but much smaller in size.

definition_vi: Một biểu đồ vẽ mối quan hệ giữa độ sáng tuyệt đối và loại phổ (hay nhiệt độ) của các ngôi sao, công cụ quan trọng trong thiên văn học.
definition_en: A diagram plotting the relationship between stellar luminosity and spectral type, fundamental to stellar classification.
example_sentence: The Hertzsprung-Russell diagram reveals the evolutionary stages of stars from birth to death.

definition_vi: Giai đoạn chính của cuộc đời một ngôi sao, khi nó đốt cháy hydro trong lõi, kéo dài hàng tỷ năm.
definition_en: The primary stage of a star's life during which it fuses hydrogen in its core.
example_sentence: Our Sun is currently in the main sequence stage and will remain there for approximately 5 billion more years.

definition_vi: câu chuyện, tường thuật
definition_en: a story or account of events and experiences
example_sentence: The author's compelling narrative about his childhood struggles captivated millions of readers worldwide.

definition_vi: nhân vật chính
definition_en: the main character in a story or play
example_sentence: The protagonist's internal conflict drives the entire plot forward throughout the novel.

definition_vi: văn xuôi, văn bản thường
definition_en: written or spoken language in its ordinary form, not poetry
example_sentence: Modern prose fiction has evolved significantly since the nineteenth century with new stylistic innovations.

definition_vi: ẩn dụ, so sánh
definition_en: a figure of speech in which a word or phrase is applied to something it doesn't literally denote
example_sentence: Shakespeare's use of extended metaphor comparing life to a stage demonstrates remarkable literary sophistication.

definition_vi: tính biểu tượng, chủ nghĩa tượng trưng
definition_en: the use of symbols to represent ideas or qualities in art and literature
example_sentence: The symbolism of the white whale in Moby Dick has been extensively analysed by literary scholars.

definition_vi: phương ngữ, lối nói
definition_en: a particular form of a language spoken in a specific region or by a particular group
example_sentence: The author masterfully employs regional dialect to establish authentic character voices throughout the narrative.

definition_vi: cao trào, điểm cao nhất
definition_en: the most intense or exciting point in a story or dramatic work
example_sentence: The climax of the novel occurs when the protagonist finally discovers the truth about his origins.

definition_vi: sơ thảo trước, báo hiệu trước
definition_en: to suggest or indicate something beforehand through hints or clues
example_sentence: The writer foreshadows the tragic ending through subtle symbolic details in the opening chapters.

definition_vi: tính châm chọc, mỉa mai, trớ trêu
definition_en: the use of language to convey meaning by using language that signifies the opposite
example_sentence: The irony in O. Henry's stories often leaves readers surprised by unexpected plot twists.

definition_vi: ám chỉ, gợi ý gián tiếp
definition_en: an indirect or passing reference to something without mentioning it explicitly
example_sentence: The poet's allusion to classical mythology enriches the meaning of the contemporary verse.

definition_vi: thẩm mỹ, liên quan đến cái đẹp
definition_en: concerning beauty or the appreciation of beauty in art and literature
example_sentence: The novel's aesthetic qualities are evident in the author's careful attention to vivid imagery and style.

definition_vi: tu từ, nghệ thuật thuyết phục
definition_en: the art of effective or persuasive speaking and writing using language devices
example_sentence: The effectiveness of Churchill's rhetoric during wartime inspired and motivated millions of people.

definition_vi: kết thúc, hồi kết của truyện
definition_en: the final part of a narrative in which mysteries are resolved and loose ends tied up
example_sentence: The denouement of the detective novel satisfactorily resolves all the plot threads established earlier.

definition_vi: đặt cạnh nhau để so sánh
definition_en: to place two contrasting things side by side for comparative effect
example_sentence: The author juxtaposes scenes of poverty and wealth to highlight social inequality in the society.

definition_vi: tính mơ hồ, không rõ ràng
definition_en: the quality of being unclear or capable of being understood in multiple ways
example_sentence: The ambiguity in the poem's final stanza invites readers to interpret its meaning differently.

definition_vi: chơi chữ, ẩn dụ
definition_en: a joke exploiting different possible meanings of a word or words with similar sounds
example_sentence: Shakespeare's comedies are filled with clever puns that entertain audiences across centuries.

definition_vi: sống động, sinh động
definition_en: producing strong, clear images in the mind; striking and memorable
example_sentence: The author's vivid descriptions of the landscape transport readers directly into the story's setting.

definition_vi: cảm động, chạm đến trái tim
definition_en: evoking a keen sense of sadness or regret; deeply moving or touching
example_sentence: The poignant scene of farewell between the elderly couple moved audiences to tears.

definition_vi: truyện ngụ ngôn, biểu tượng
definition_en: a story or poem in which characters and events are symbols representing abstract ideas
example_sentence: George Orwell's Animal Farm is a political allegory that critiques totalitarianism and corruption.

definition_vi: tô vẽ nhân vật,刻画人物
definition_en: the way characters are portrayed and developed in a literary work
example_sentence: The character's psychological development through skilful characterisation reveals layers of complexity.

definition_vi: suy luận, suy ra
definition_en: to reach a conclusion based on evidence and reasoning rather than explicit statement
example_sentence: Readers must infer the protagonist's true motives from subtle clues scattered throughout the narrative.

definition_vi: lời nói đầu, mở đầu
definition_en: an introductory section of a book, play, or film that precedes the main narrative
example_sentence: The prologue effectively sets the historical context before the main events unfold.

definition_vi: điều tục tĩu, cái được lặp lại nhiều lần
definition_en: a phrase or idea that has been overused and has lost its original impact or freshness
example_sentence: Good writers avoid tired clichés and instead develop unique perspectives on universal human experiences.

definition_vi: liên quan đến chủ đề
definition_en: relating to or based on the main subject or theme of something
example_sentence: The thematic significance of water as a symbol of rebirth appears repeatedly throughout the novel.

definition_vi: kỹ thuật tường thuật, cách kể chuyện
definition_en: the specific methods and devices an author uses to tell a story effectively
example_sentence: The author's innovative narrative technique employs multiple perspectives to deepen thematic exploration.

definition_vi: mỉa mai, châm chọc
definition_en: using humour and irony to criticize people, institutions, or social conventions
example_sentence: Swift's satirical essay brilliantly mocks social prejudices through exaggerated and absurd proposals.

definition_vi: có sắc thái tinh tế, tinh vi
definition_en: showing subtle differences and fine distinctions; complex and sophisticated
example_sentence: The nuanced portrayal of the character's emotional journey demonstrates exceptional psychological insight.

definition_vi: phơi bày, giải thích chi tiết
definition_en: the presentation of information that readers need to understand the context and background
example_sentence: The exposition in the opening chapter establishes the historical setting and introduces major characters.

definition_vi: gợi lên, kích thích tưởng tượng
definition_en: evoking or capable of evoking strong feelings, images, or memories
example_sentence: The poet's evocative language creates powerful sensory experiences that resonate with readers.

definition_vi: giá trị văn học, mức độ sáng tạo nghệ thuật
definition_en: the quality and artistic value of a piece of writing based on its style, themes, and impact
example_sentence: The novel's literary merit has been recognized by prestigious literary awards and academic institutions.

definition_vi: Một chủ đề, hình ảnh hoặc ý tưởng được lặp lại nhiều lần trong một tác phẩm văn học để tạo ra ý nghĩa hoặc hiệu ứng thẩm mỹ.
definition_en: A recurring element, image, or idea in a literary work that contributes to its overall meaning or artistic effect.
example_sentence: The motif of water appears throughout the novel, symbolizing both rebirth and destruction in the protagonist's journey.

definition_vi: Kỹ thuật viết mô tả dòng suy nghĩ liên tục, không được sắp xếp logic, của một nhân vật, thường bao gồm cả những suy nghĩ vô thức.
definition_en: A narrative technique that portrays the continuous flow of a character's thoughts and feelings, often unstructured and including subconscious elements.
example_sentence: James Joyce's Ulysses employs stream of consciousness to reveal the inner workings of Leopold Bloom's mind throughout a single day.

definition_vi: Một người kể chuyện mà lời kể của họ không đáng tin cậy do sự thiên vị, điên rồ, hoặc cố ý che giấu sự thật.
definition_en: A narrator whose account of events cannot be fully trusted due to bias, mental instability, or deliberate deception.
example_sentence: The unreliable narrator in Gone Girl keeps readers guessing about the truth, making the twist ending particularly shocking.

definition_vi: Chất lượng của một tác phẩm văn học hoặc nghệ thuật có vẻ như thực tế hoặc có thể xảy ra, mặc dù nó có thể là hư cấu.
definition_en: The quality of appearing to be true or real, even if the work is fictional; the appearance of authenticity.
example_sentence: The author's meticulous attention to historical detail lends verisimilitude to the narrative, making readers believe in the fictional world.
